purpose: Per-parser-family AST-shape contract for the SystemVerilog grammar. The generated SV parser is NOT in the default `cargo test --features generated_parsers` build; it is produced on-demand by `sv_stimuli_quality_gate` (and similar) into `rust/target/<gate>/work/systemverilog_parser.rs` and discarded. The cfg `has_generated_systemverilog_parser` therefore stays off in default builds and the matching unit test compiles out. When the parser is present (gate run or `PGEN_SYSTEMVERILOG_PARSER_PATH` override), the test activates and the contract is enforced.
doctrine: Calibrated 2026-05-04 against a freshly-generated parser (parser release 1.0.1, contract 1.0.1). On first run with the parser available, this manifest is expected to match — `current_content_kind: "json_object"` was confirmed by running `parseability_probe --parse-dump-ast-pretty systemverilog /tmp/sv_calibration/minimal_module.sv` after the SV-Slice-1 grammar fix that moved the dangling `systemverilog_file` annotation onto the rule it was supposed to attach to. Future drift fails the regression-lock and surfaces the change to the slice that introduced it.
calibration_history: 2026-05-05 SV-Slice-22 (batch): generate sub-tree typed (3 rules / 7 annotations) — closes the generate-construct walk path. (a) `generate_region` typed `{items: $2}` (drops kw_generate / kw_endgenerate; surfaces clean items array). (b) `generate_item` 3 kinds (module_or_generate_item / interface_or_generate_item / checker_or_generate_item) — uniform `{kind, body}` dispatch on which generate-item form was matched. (c) `generate_block` 3 kinds: `"anonymous"` (`{label, items, end_label}` for `begin ... end` form), `"labeled"` (`{name, label, items, end_label}` for `name : begin ... end` form), `"generate_item"` (`{body}` for the bare-generate_item passthrough — no begin/end wrapping). The optional inner `( colon generate_block_identifier )?` after kw_begin captures the block-internal label slot (or `[]` when absent); the trailing optional captures the end-label slot. Cascading walk impact: every `non_port_module_item.kind=='generate_region'` and every `module_common_item.kind=='loop_generate_construct'`/`'conditional_generate_construct'` (when those typed rules eventually resolve through generate_region or generate_block) now exposes typed dispatch into the generate body. Annotation count: 225 (was 218, +7). Same accept set.

2026-05-05 SV-Slice-21 (batch): module_common_item + package_or_generate_item_declaration typed (4 rules / 55 annotations — biggest batch yet). (a) `module_common_item_sv_2017` 13 kinds (module_or_generate_item_declaration / interface_instantiation / program_instantiation / assertion_item / bind_directive / continuous_assign / net_alias / initial_construct / final_construct / always_construct / loop_generate_construct / conditional_generate_construct / elaboration_system_task) — uniform `{kind, body}` shape. (b) `module_common_item_sv_2023` 13 kinds (same as sv_2017 except `elaboration_severity_system_task` instead of `elaboration_system_task` — LRM 2023 system-task expansion). (c) `package_or_generate_item_declaration_sv_2017` 14 kinds (local_parameter_declaration / parameter_declaration / net_declaration / dpi_import_export / data_declaration / task_declaration / function_declaration / checker_declaration / extern_constraint_declaration / class_declaration / class_constructor_declaration / covergroup_declaration / assertion_item_declaration / semi) — `local_parameter_declaration` and `parameter_declaration` drop trailing semi via `body: $1`; `semi` is bare with no body. (d) `package_or_generate_item_declaration_sv_2023` 15 kinds (same plus `interface_class_declaration` between class_declaration and class_constructor_declaration — LRM 2023 interface-class addition). The `module_common_item` and `package_or_generate_item_declaration` profile-tag wrapper rules stay un-annotated (transparent profile-routers, same pattern as `module_declaration`/`interface_declaration`). Cascading walk impact: every `module_or_generate_item.kind=='module_common_item'` and every `non_port_program_item.kind=='module_or_generate_item_declaration'` now exposes typed dispatch through 6+ levels. Annotation count: 218 (was 163, +55). Same accept set.

2026-05-05 SV-Slice-20 (batch): interface + program items dispatch tree typed (5 rules / 19 annotations) — interface/program counterparts of SV-Slice-19's module-items batch. (a) `interface_item` 2 kinds (port_declaration / non_port_item). (b) `interface_or_generate_item` 2 kinds (module_common_item / extern_tf_declaration) — each preserves attribute_instance* prefix. (c) `non_port_interface_item` 6 kinds (generate_region / interface_or_generate / program_declaration / modport_declaration / interface_declaration / timeunits_declaration). (d) `program_item` 2 kinds (port_declaration / non_port_item). (e) `non_port_program_item` 7 kinds (continuous_assign / module_or_generate_item_declaration / initial_construct / final_construct / concurrent_assertion_item / timeunits_declaration / program_generate_item) — first 5 preserve attribute_instance* prefix. The interface and program declaration sub-trees now match the module sub-tree's level of typed dispatch — every `header.items` / `body.items` field on every typed interface/program declaration surfaces kind-discriminated dispatch into actual content. Annotation count: 163 (was 144, +19). Same accept set.

2026-05-05 SV-Slice-19 (batch): module-items dispatch tree typed (5 rules / 22 annotations). (a) `module_item` 2 kinds (port_declaration / non_port_item). (b) `module_or_generate_item` 5 kinds (parameter_override / gate_instantiation / udp_instantiation / module_instantiation / module_common_item) — each preserves attribute_instance* prefix. (c) `module_or_generate_item_declaration` 5 kinds (package_or_generate / genvar / clocking / default_clocking / default_disable_iff). (d) `non_port_module_item` 8 kinds (generate_region / module_or_generate / specify_block / specparam_declaration / program_declaration / module_declaration / interface_declaration / timeunits_declaration). (e) `continuous_assign` 2 kinds (net / variable). All `header.items` and `body.items` fields on every typed module/interface/program declaration now surface kind-discriminated dispatch into the actual content. Annotation count: 144 (was 122, +22). Same accept set.

2026-05-05 SV-Slice-18 (batch): UDP truth-table entry rules typed (3 rules / 3 annotations). (a) `combinational_entry` typed `{inputs, output}` (drops colon/semi). (b) `sequential_entry` typed `{inputs, current_state, next_state}` (drops colons/semi). (c) `udp_initial_statement` typed `{name, init_val}` (drops kw_initial/assign/semi). All UDP truth-table rows now have clean typed shapes; consumers walk inputs/output/state-transitions directly without descending the raw envelope. Annotation count: 122 (was 119, +3). Same accept set.

2026-05-05 SV-Slice-17 (batch): UDP body sub-tree typed (6 rules / 8 annotations). (a) `udp_body` 2 kinds (combinational/sequential). (b) `udp_input_declaration` typed `{attributes, identifiers}` (drops kw_input). (c) `udp_output_declaration` 2 kinds (wire / reg with optional default). (d) `combinational_body` typed `{entries: {first, rest}}` (drops kw_table/kw_endtable). (e) `sequential_body` typed `{initial, entries: {first, rest}}` (preserves optional initial statement). (f) `list_of_udp_port_identifiers` typed `{first, rest}` (mini-mixed-array). All UDP declaration internals now typed end-to-end. Annotation count: 119 (was 111, +8). Same accept set.

2026-05-05 SV-Slice-16 (batch): port + port_direction + package_import family typed (9 annotations across 4 rules). (a) `port` 2 kinds (expression / named) — discriminates positional vs named-dot port forms. (b) `port_direction` 4 kinds (input/output/inout/ref). (c) `package_import_declaration` `{items: {first, rest}}` — drops kw_import/semi. (d) `package_import_item` 2 kinds (explicit with `{package, name}` / wildcard with `{package}` only). DEFERRED: `ansi_port_declaration` 3-branch typing — task #38 (parens-grouped-Or trailing-annotation attribution bug) blocks it; branch 0 starts with `( net_port_header | interface_port_header )?` which causes branch-index renumbering and lost annotation registration. Would need either task #38 fix or grammar refactor (extract parens-Or into a named helper rule). Annotation count: 111 (was 102, +9). Same accept set.

2026-05-05 SV-Slice-15 (batch): port-list family + small structural rules typed (6 rules, 7 annotations). (a) `list_of_ports` typed `{first, rest}` (mini-mixed-array; drops parens). (b) `list_of_port_declarations` transparent passthrough `-> $2` (drops parens, surfaces optional inner content). (c) `udp_port_list` typed `{output, inputs: {first, rest}}` (output_port_identifier + input list with mini-mixed-array). (d) `udp_declaration_port_list` parallel shape `{output, inputs: {first, rest}}` for ANSI form. (e) `anonymous_program` typed `{items}` (drops kw_program/semi/kw_endprogram). (f) `package_export_declaration` 2 kinds (wildcard / explicit with items: {first, rest}). All `header.ports` fields on module/interface/program/UDP declarations now surface typed shapes. Annotation count: 102 (was 95, +7). Same accept set.

2026-05-05 SV-Slice-14 (batch): 5 rules typed completing bind sub-tree + interface_class_declaration + config_declaration. (a) `bind_target_scope` 2 kinds (module/interface) — uniform `{kind, name}` shape. (b) `bind_target_instance` typed `{name, bit_select}`. (c) `bind_target_instance_list` typed via {first, rest} mini-mixed-array workaround for `bind_target_instance ( comma bind_target_instance )*` repetition. (d) `interface_class_declaration` typed `{name, parameters, extends, items, end_label}` (single sequence). (e) `config_declaration` typed `{name, local_params, design, rules, end_label}` (single sequence). Annotation count: 95 (was 89, +6). Same accept set.

2026-05-05 SV-Slice-13 (batch): bind_directive + bind_instantiation + package_item per-branch typed. (a) `bind_directive` 2 kinds (scoped/single) — scoped form preserves target_scope, optional instances, instantiation; single form preserves target_instance, instantiation. (b) `bind_instantiation` 4 kinds (program/module/interface/checker) — uniform `{kind, body}` shape over the 4 instantiation forms. (c) `package_item` 4 kinds (declaration/anonymous_program/export/timeunits) — discriminates the 4 forms reachable inside `description.body` when description.kind=='package_item'. Annotation count: 89 (was 79, +10). Same accept set.

2026-05-05 SV-Slice-12 (batch): UDP declaration family typed (4 rules; mirrors module/interface/program structural pattern with one wrinkle). (a) `udp_ansi_declaration` typed `{attributes, name, ports}` (drops kw_primitive, parens, semi). (b) `udp_nonansi_declaration` same field names (only ports source rule differs). (c) `udp_declaration_sv_2017` 5 per-branch kinds (nonansi/ansi/extern_nonansi/extern_ansi/wildcard). (d) `udp_declaration_sv_2023` mirror with positional shift in wildcard. **Special: nonansi branch has `udp_port_declaration udp_port_declaration*` mini-mixed-array** (a required first + repeat) — used `port_decls: {first: $2, rest: $3}` workaround (same idiom as attribute_instance from slice 6). Annotation count: 79 (was 67, +12). Same accept set.

2026-05-05 SV-Slice-11 (batch): program-header sub-tree typed (mirror of module/interface header pattern). (a) `program_ansi_header` typed `{attributes, lifetime, name, imports, parameters, ports}`. (b) `program_nonansi_header` same field names (only ports source rule differs — list_of_ports vs (list_of_port_declarations)?). For `program p; endprogram\n`: header.name = 'p' (clean string from slice 8), all 6 named fields populated. Annotation count: 67 (was 65, +2). Same accept set.

2026-05-05 SV-Slice-10 (batch): class + package + program declarations typed. (a) `class_declaration_sv_2017` — single sequence typed `{virtual, lifetime, name, parameters, extends, implements, items, end_label}`. (b) `class_declaration_sv_2023` — same shape with `final_specifier` instead of `lifetime` (semantically distinct field per LRM 2023). (c) `package_declaration` — single sequence typed `{attributes, lifetime, name, timeunits, items, end_label}` (annotation registered; runtime parse on bare `package p; endpackage` input fails at position 0 — likely pre-existing issue, not introduced by this slice; module/interface/program tests still pass with same parser). (d) `program_declaration_sv_2017` — 5 per-branch kinds (nonansi/ansi/wildcard/extern_nonansi/extern_ansi). NOTE: program puts nonansi BEFORE ansi in the rule definition (different from module/interface) — kind labels still discriminate correctly. (e) `program_declaration_sv_2023` — same 5 kinds with positional shift in wildcard. Verified empirically on `program p; endprogram\n`: source_text[0].body.body.kind = 'ansi'. Annotation count: 65 (was 53, +12). Same accept set across all annotated rules.

2026-05-04 SV-Slice-9 (batch): interface declarations typed — full mirror of the module pattern. (a) `interface_ansi_header` typed `{attributes, lifetime, name, imports, parameters, ports}` (no keyword field — only one keyword exists for interfaces). (b) `interface_nonansi_header` same field names. (c) `interface_declaration_sv_2017` 5 per-branch kinds (ansi/nonansi/wildcard/extern_nonansi/extern_ansi). (d) `interface_declaration_sv_2023` same kind set with positional shift in wildcard. Verified empirically on `interface bus; endinterface\n`: source_text[0].body.body.kind='ansi', header.name='bus' (clean string, inherited from slice 8 identifier typing). Annotation count: 53 (was 41, +12). Same accept set.

2026-05-04 SV-Slice-8 (batch): four identifier-leaf rules typed (`-> $2` to drop trivia + surface the regex-captured identifier name as a clean string). (a) `simple_identifier := trivia /[a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_$]*/ -> $2`. (b) `escaped_identifier := trivia /\\[!-~]+/ -> $2`. (c) `non_keyword_identifier := !reserved_non_keyword_identifier identifier -> $2`. (d) `simple_identifier_no_scope := trivia /[a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_$]*(?![ \t\r\n]*::)/ -> $2`. **Massive propagation effect**: these are leaf rules consumed by `identifier`, `declaration_identifier`, `module_identifier`, `class_identifier`, `package_identifier`, `interface_identifier`, etc. — each of which is a transparent passthrough alias. After this slice, ALL identifier-typed fields (anywhere in any rule that ultimately resolves to an identifier) surface as clean JSON strings instead of the raw envelope chain. For `module m; endmodule\n`: `header.name` was `[[], [[], "m"]]` (raw envelope) → now `"m"` (clean string). Annotation count: 41 (was 37, +4). Same accept set.

2026-05-04 SV-Slice-7 (batch): four rules typed in one slice. (a) `module_keyword` per-branch typed (`{kind: "module"}` / `{kind: "macromodule"}`). (b) `lifetime` per-branch typed (`{kind: "static"}` / `{kind: "automatic"}`). (c) `module_ansi_header` typed `{attributes, keyword, lifetime, name, imports, parameters, ports}`. (d) `module_nonansi_header` typed with same field names (only `ports` source differs — `list_of_ports` vs `(list_of_port_declarations)?`). For `module m; endmodule\n`: the previously-raw `header:` field of the ansi-kind module_declaration_sv_2017 now resolves to a typed object with `keyword: {kind: "module"}` (via slice 7a) and named optional/quantified fields. **Four layers of typed dispatch now end-to-end**: source_text_item.kind → description.kind → module_declaration_sv_<profile>.kind → module_<form>_header (with sub-typed keyword). Annotation count: 37 (was 31, +6: 2 module_keyword, 2 lifetime, 1 module_ansi_header, 1 module_nonansi_header). Same accept set across all four slices.

2026-05-04 SV-Slice-6 (batch): three rules typed in one slice. (a) `attribute_instance` annotated `{first: $2, rest: $3}` — drops attr_open/close delimiters, exposes first attr_spec + trailing reps. (b) `module_declaration_sv_2017` per-branch typed (5 branches: ansi/nonansi/wildcard/extern_nonansi/extern_ansi) with kind discriminator and structured fields (header/timeunits/items/end_label, plus attributes/keyword/lifetime/name on the wildcard branch). (c) `module_declaration_sv_2023` same per-branch typing as sv_2017 (kind set is identical; only the wildcard branch's positional indices differ — sv_2023 uses `dot star` (2 tokens) vs sv_2017's `dot_star` (1 token), shifting timeunits from $9→$10, items from $10→$11, end_label from $12→$13). Three layers of typed dispatch now end-to-end: source_text_item.kind → description.kind → module_declaration_sv_<profile>.kind. For `module m; endmodule\n` (sv_2017 profile): source_text[0].body.body now resolves to `{kind: "ansi", header: <module_ansi_header envelope>, timeunits: [], items: [], end_label: []}`. Annotation count: 31 (was 20). NOTE: `comment_only_source_region` typing was attempted in this batch but BLOCKED by task #38 (parens-grouped-Or trailing-annotation attribution bug); annotation didn't register and was reverted. Same accept set across all three slices.

2026-05-04 SV-Slice-5: `compiler_directive` transparent passthrough of directive text (`-> $2`). For an input with `` `define FOO bar `` followed by `module m; endmodule\n`, source_text now has 2 items: item[0] is `{kind: "compiler_directive", body: "`define FOO bar"}` (body is now a clean string instead of nested envelope), item[1] is the typed module description. Drops the `trivia` prefix slot from the matched sequence; emits just the captured directive text. Annotation count: 20 (was 19). Same accept set; only the compiler_directive shape changed.

2026-05-04 SV-Slice-4: `description` per-branch typed (8 branches). Each Or branch annotated. Branches 1-5 + 8 (single-element): `{kind: "<branch_name>", body: $1}`. Branches 6-7 (multi-element `attribute_instance* X`): `{kind: "<branch_name>", attributes: $1, body: $2}` — preserves attribute_instance* prefix as a separate `attributes` field. For the `module m; endmodule\n` sample, source_text[0].body is now `{"kind": "module_declaration", "body": <envelope>}` (was raw envelope). Two layers of typed dispatch now end-to-end (source_text_item.kind → description.kind). Annotation count: 19 (was 11). Same accept set.

2026-05-04 SV-Slice-3: `source_text_item` per-branch typed (8 branches). Each Or branch annotated with `{kind: "<branch_name>", body: $1}` — kinds: description, local_parameter_declaration, parameter_declaration, package_import_declaration, timeunits_declaration, compiler_directive, comment_only_source_region, semi (the last carries no body since it's just `;`). Pre-fix each source_text_item carried the matched-branch shape directly without a discriminator; post-fix consumers can dispatch on `item["kind"]`. For the `module m; endmodule\n` sample, source_text[0] is now `{"kind": "description", "body": <description envelope>}`. Annotation count: 11 (was 3). Same accept set.

2026-05-04 SV-Slice-2: `source_text := source_text_item*` annotated `-> [$1**]`. Pre-fix `source_text` field of `systemverilog_file` was a nested Quantified envelope; post-fix it's a flat array of `source_text_item` shapes. For the `module m; endmodule\n` sample, source_text now has length 1 (the single module_declaration's source_text_item envelope) instead of the Quantified wrapper. Annotation count: 3 (was 2). Same accept set; only the source_text array shape changed.

2026-05-04 SV-Slice-1: dangling annotation `-> {type: "systemverilog_file", source_text: $2}` at grammars/systemverilog.ebnf line 195 was moved onto its target rule `systemverilog_file` (line 184). Pre-fix the annotation was lost (separated from its rule by a blank line + the `sv_multi_entry_root` helper definition + another blank line) and the parser registered 0 annotations effective on systemverilog_file. Post-fix the parser registers 2 annotations (systemverilog_file + systemverilog_parseable_file) and the AST root for `module m; endmodule\n` is the typed `{type: "systemverilog_file", source_text: ...}` JSON object documented below.

samples.name: minimal_module
samples.drift_tracked_in: SV-Slice-22 batch (parser release 1.0.22 / contract 1.0.22) — generate sub-tree typed (3 rules / 7 annotations). minimal_module sample is unaffected (no items). See docs/contracts/PGEN_SYSTEMVERILOG_PARSER_INTEGRATION_CONTRACT.md § "Release 1.0.22 / Contract 1.0.22 Highlights".
